revision.text:
[[ja:茶臼山古墳 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Chausuyama (Osaka), zenkei.jpg|location={{ill|Q86727104|qid=Q86727104|1=Q86727104}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.651683|N|135.512339|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|five lowest mountains in Osaka|qid=Q97188165|1=five lowest mountains in Osaka}}}} {{infobox kofun | name = {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun (Osaka)|ja|茶臼山古墳 (大阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun (Osaka)|qq=}} | location = [[Osaka Prefecture|{{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}]], [[Osaka City|{{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}]], [[Tennoji Ward|{{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|qid=Q490592|lt=Tennoji Ward|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}]], Chausuyama-cho 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|39|6.06|N|135|30|44.42|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Chausuyama Kofun (Osaka)|qq=}} | type = [[Zenpokoen-fun|{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) | built = [[5th century|{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}]] | area = 200m mound length | Site designation1 = Osaka Prefectural Historic Site ("Chausuyama Kofun and Kawazoko Pond") | image = [[File:Chausuyama (Osaka), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound
(Left: presumed rear circular section, Right: presumed front rectangular section, Foreground: Kawazoko Pond) | notes = }} '''{{ill|Chausuyama Kofun (Osaka)|ja|茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun (Osaka)|qq=}}''' (ちゃうすやまこふん) is a '''keyhole-shaped Kofun''' located in '''{{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|qid=Q490592|lt=Tennoji Ward|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan'''. It is Historic Site1 as an '''Osaka Prefectural Historic Site'''. == Overview == The area surrounding the '''Chausuyama Kofun''' is part of '''{{ill|Tennōji Park|en|Tennōji Park|qid=Q5364342|lt=Tennoji Park|1=Tennōji Park}}''', and the Kofun itself has been integrated into the park with '''walking paths''' developed around it. Separating the kofun from the main part of the park is '''Kawazoko Pond''' (かわぞこいけ, also known as こそこいけ or intypeally '''"Chabuike"'''). According to one theory, the pond is a remnant of an '''unsuccessful canal excavation attempt in 788 (Enryaku 7)''' by '''{{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|qid=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}}''', who tried to carve through the '''{{ill|Uemachi Plateau|en|Uemachi Plateau|qid=Q7877450|lt=Uemachi Plateau|1=Uemachi Plateau}}''' to facilitate drainage from the '''{{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|qid=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}}''' and '''{{ill|Kawachi Bay|en|Kawachi Bay|qid=Q11553429|lt=Kawachi Lake|1=Kawachi Bay}}''' for improved '''water transport'''. There is '''disagreement''' over whether the site is '''actually a kofun'''. Historically, the area was known as '''Arano Misasagi (荒陵, "Wilderness Mausoleum")''' or '''Aranozan (荒陵山, "Wilderness Hill")''', and it was long believed to be an ancient Kofun. A '''Sarcophagus lid''', supposedly excavated from the site, has been preserved at '''{{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|qid=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}'''. Additionally, '''historical maps''' suggest a mound structure, which has supported the long-standing classification as a '''zenpokoen-fun (keyhole-shaped Kofun)'''. However, '''excavations conducted in 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' failed to uncover '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (Kofun paving stones), leading some scholars to dispute its classification as a kofun[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009784.html Osaka City Events & Tourism - Chausuyama Kofun, Municipal Museum & Keitaku-en]. On the other hand, '''Shitennō-ji''''s grounds have yielded '''haniwa burial goods''', suggesting the area might still be significant for ancient burials. Further excavations of Chausuyama and its surroundings are awaited. In '''2009 (Heisei 21)''', an '''excavation revealed what appeared to be a stone chamber coated with cinnabar (mercury sulfide red pigment)''', renewing discussions about its possible kofun status. The mound’s '''elevation is 26m''', as recorded by the '''Geospatial Intypeation Authority of Japan (GSI)''', and it is considered one of the '''Osaka Five Low Mountains (大阪五低山)'''. == History == The kofun is believed to have been '''constructed in the 5th century''' as a burial site for a '''local chieftain'''. It is one of the '''largest keyhole tombs in Osaka City''', but the identity of the '''buried individual remains unknown'''. During later centuries, the site saw military use: * In '''1546 (Tenmon 15)''', '''{{ill|Hosokawa Harumoto|en|Hosokawa Harumoto|qid=Q859769|lt=Hosokawa Harumoto|1=Hosokawa Harumoto}}'s retainer, {{ill|Yamanaka Matazaburō|ja|山中又三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanaka Matazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', constructed '''Ōtsuka Castle (大塚城)''' on the rear circular portion of the mound. * In '''1614 (Keichō 19)''', during the '''Winter Campaign of the Siege of Osaka''', '''Chausuyama''' served as the '''main encampment of Tokugawa Ieyasu'''. * In '''1615 (Keichō 20)''', during the '''Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka''', '''Sanada Nobushige (Yukimura)''' made '''Chausuyama his base''', leading to the famous '''Battle of Chausuyama''', part of the '''Battle of Tennōji & Okayama'''. In the '''Meiji era''', the '''Sumitomo family''' acquired the area as part of their estate. However, in '''1925 (Taishō 14)''', the '''Sumitomo family donated the land''', including the site of their residence (now the '''Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts''') and '''Keitaku-en Garden''', to '''Osaka City''', which then incorporated it into '''Tennoji Park'''. '''Kawazoko Pond was officially added to the park in 1928 (Shōwa 3)'''. File:Chausuyama02s3200.jpg|Mound and Kawazoko Pond File:Chausuyama (Osaka), sanchou.jpg|Mound summit File:Chausuyama kofun.jpg|Aerial view == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Hōrikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrikoshi Shrine|1=Horikoshi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11427801}} * {{ill|Yasui Shrine|ja|安居神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Shrine|1=Yasui Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11450228}} * {{ill|Isshin-ji|en|Isshin-ji|qid=Q3155710|lt=Isshin-ji|1=Isshin-ji}} * {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|qid=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}} ''' {{ill|Shitennō-ji Honbō Garden|ja|四天王寺本坊庭園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennō-ji Honbō Garden|1=Shitennō-ji Honbō Garden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11419826}} * {{ill|Hōfuku-ji (Tennōji)|ja|邦福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfuku-ji (Tennōji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Tōkoku-ji|ja|統国寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkoku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) == References == {{Commons|Category:Chausuyama kofun, Osaka|qq=}} {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub|qq=}} '''Chausuyama Kofun (Osaka City)''' {{translated page|ja|茶臼山古墳 (大阪市)|version=104074470|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Chausuyama kofun, Osaka}} [[ja:茶臼山古墳 (大阪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11618016}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:大坂の陣]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:天王寺公園]]
